texto: For me, the most fun is change or growth. There are definitely elements of both that I like. Launching a business is kind of like a motorboat: You can go very quickly and turn fast.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Tony Hsieh: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a negocios. O trecho “For me, the most fun is change or growth. There are definitely elements of both that I like. Laun…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que negocios, change, growth pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de mudanca e growth.

texto: I had decided to stop chasing the money, and start chasing the passion.
explicacao: Citação de Tony Hsieh sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “I had decided to stop chasing the money, and start chasing the passion.” articula reflexao, happiness, chasing em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.

frase_original: I had decided to stop chasing the money, and start chasing the passion.

texto: The typical industry approach is [retailers] to treat vendors like the enemy... If vendors can't make a profit then they don't have money to invest in research and development, which in turn means that the products they bring to the market will be less inspiring to customers, which in turn detriments the retailer's business because customers aren't inspired to buy. People want to cut costs and negotiate aggressively because there's a limited amount of profit to be shared by both sides. As a result of this "death spiral", most retailers fail.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Tony Hsieh: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “The typical industry approach is [retailers] to treat vendors like the enemy... If vendors can't …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que negocios, business, business-success pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de inspiracao e luto.

frase_original: The typical industry approach is [retailers] to treat vendors like the enemy... If vendors can't make a profit then they don't have money to invest in research and development, which in turn means that the products they bring to the market will be less inspiring to customers, which in turn detriments the retailer's business because customers aren't inspired to buy. People want to cut costs and negotiate aggressively because there's a limited amount of profit to be shared by both sides. As a result of this "death spiral", most retailers fail.
